records.ety: [Cf. OF. averement, LL. averamentum. See Aver, v. t.]

defs: The act of averring, or that which is averred; affirmation; positive assertion.
quotes: Signally has this averment received illustration in the course of recent events.

defs: A positive statement of facts; an allegation; an offer to justify or prove what is alleged.
notes: ☞ In any stage of pleadings, when either party advances new matter, he avers it to be true, by using this form of words: “and this he is ready to verify.” This was formerly called an averment. It modern pleading, it is termed a verification. Blackstone.
